dotfiles.description: Public Arch/Omarchy dotfiles with GNU Stow and a compiled dot CLI: machine bootstrap, multi-repo git tooling, GitHub workflow and notification inboxes, Omarchy desktop sync, optional private overlays, and OpenCode agent publishing.

opencode-config.description: Shared OpenCode skills, agents, plugins, and commands published from dotfiles: branch-context workflows, permission-scoped agents, refactor commands, env protection, and skills for TypeScript, codebase diagnostics, and Home Assistant frontend work.

omarchy-waybar.description: Waybar config for Omarchy with desktop and laptop layouts. Custom modules surface git notifications, workflow runs, and diff status via the dot CLI, plus Home Assistant entity watches through go-automate's bridge.

go-automate.description: Utility to run common tasks and trigger Home Assistant automations. A local Unix socket bridge multiplexes one Home Assistant WebSocket connection for entity watches and status-bar scripts, shipped as a systemd user service with Arch packaging.

system-bridge.description: Cross-platform app for Linux and Windows that exposes system information and control via an API and WebSocket server, integrating with Home Assistant for monitoring desktops and sending commands.

workflows.description: Shared GitHub Actions workflows: linting, testing, CodeQL, container and language builds, Home Assistant card validation, Dependabot and Renovate automerge, and release drafting.

weather.description: Progressive web app for current conditions and a five-day forecast powered by Tomorrow.io, with coordinate or geolocation location and dark/light themes.

pouch.description: Web app for organising lists and notepads into groups with drag-and-drop reordering and real-time sync, built with Next.js, Convex, and Clerk auth.

home-panel.description: Archived web frontend for controlling the home, integrated with Home Assistant as an additional frontend. No longer maintained.

go-commands.description: Archived CLI to run commands on your machine via MQTT and Home Assistant, triggering shell commands remotely from automations.

home-assistant-tui.description: Terminal UI for Home Assistant built with OpenTUI and Effect. Browse entities by domain and area, fuzzy search, and view favourites from your Home Assistant frontend config.

ha-dashboard-maintenance.description: Home Assistant dashboard (HACS) for low batteries, repairs, updates, offline devices, and stale sensors, with room-by-room views and a summary tile.

ha-card-calendar-agenda.description: Home Assistant card (HACS) showing upcoming calendar events in a list, with multi-calendar selection, look-ahead window, and optional duplicate collapsing.

ha-card-energy-breakdown.description: Home Assistant card (HACS) showing current power usage broken down by room and device, from a whole-home view down to individual entities.

ha-card-tile-popup.description: Home Assistant card (HACS): a dashboard tile that opens a panel with more cards inside, for hiding controls or detail behind a single tap.

ha-card-anchor.description: Home Assistant card (HACS) that links to a specific place on a long dashboard, opening a URL with an anchor to jump to a section.

ha-card-feature-weather-forecast.description: Home Assistant tile card feature (HACS) adding a forecast strip to weather entities, with configurable period count and temperature rounding.

ha-card-feature-state.description: Home Assistant tile card feature (HACS) showing an entity's state, attributes, or extra detail like area name, with adjustable text size.

homeassistant-integration-goxlr-utility.description: Archived Home Assistant integration (HACS) connecting GoXLR Utility: binary sensors, media players with volume control, and profile sensors.

home-assistant-frontend.description: Home Assistant frontend (Lit web components, TypeScript). Timmo is a core maintainer; reference for HA frontend implementation details and conventions.

home-assistant-core.description: Home Assistant core (Python). Home of the contributed integrations referenced by the site: System Bridge, Honeywell Lyric, OVO Energy, Azure DevOps, and GitHub.
